message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) to outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: add optional 'filters' param to @prompt decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...)), update Prompt.from_str/from_file to accept filters, add create_jinja_env() helper. Only touches outlines/prompts.py. If your feature also touches this file, let's split by function/section or take turns - tell me what you need to change.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Our features both need outlines/prompts.py and are complementary (yours: filters param on @prompt/from_str/from_file + create_jinja_env() helper; mine: global register_filter/unregister_filter registry that merges into whatever env-building code exists, local filters taking precedence). Propose: you go FIRST since your create_jinja_env() helper is the natural integration point I'll build on. Please implement your part, then send DONE with a summary of the final function signatures (esp. create_jinja_env). I'll wait, pull your changes, then add the registry on top touching only the filter-merging logic, without altering your filters-param API. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both only have this one file, so full disjointness isn't possible. Proposal to minimize collision: I'll add a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper that centralizes env creation + built-in filter registration, and wire it into the prompt decorator (supporting @prompt and @prompt(filters=...)), Prompt.from_str, and Prompt.from_file. I will NOT add any global registry state. Please implement register_filter/unregister_filter as a separate module-level dict (e.g. _CUSTOM_FILTERS) and functions placed in their own section of the file, without editing the from_str/from_file/prompt decorator signatures I'm changing. If you want your registry auto-applied inside create_jinja_env, do that merge as an independent, additive lookup at the end of that function (append your registry's filters into env.filters after mine) so our diffs touch adjacent but distinct lines rather than the same ones. Let me know if this works or if you need a different split.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed - I'll implement create_jinja_env(filters=None) + prompt decorator/from_str/from_file changes, then send DONE with exact final signatures so you can layer the registry merge on top without touching my lines.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed. You implement create_jinja_env(filters=None) + wire into prompt decorator/from_str/from_file first. I'll add _CUSTOM_FILTERS registry + register_filter/unregister_filter in a separate section, and merge the registry into create_jinja_env's env.filters at the end of that function (registry filters applied first, then your local  param applied after/on top so local still wins). I will not touch the prompt decorator/from_str/from_file signatures you're changing. Send DONE when ready and I'll pull + implement on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed (resending, previous message had a shell backtick glitch). You implement create_jinja_env(filters=None) and wire it into the prompt decorator, from_str, and from_file first. I will add a _CUSTOM_FILTERS registry plus register_filter/unregister_filter functions in a separate section, and merge the registry into create_jinja_env env.filters at the end of that function (registry filters applied first, then your local filters param applied after so local still wins). I will not touch the prompt decorator, from_str, or from_file signatures you are changing. Send DONE when ready and I will pull and implement on top.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Done. Final signatures: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment — builds env with loader, registers built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters truthy at the END of the function (last 2 lines before 'return env'). Prompt._template_from_str(_, content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(_, path, filters=None) both just call create_jinja_env(...) and no longer build jinja2.Environment directly. Prompt.from_str/from_file now take an optional filters param and pass through. prompt() decorator signature is now prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) -> supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}), via inner decorator(fn) closure. For your registry merge: safest spot is right after my 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)' line in create_jinja_env - add your global-registry merge there (decide precedence order with per-call filters as you see fit), that's the only place you should need to touch. All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass. Good luck!

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented. Note: my sandbox's copy of outlines/prompts.py never showed your edits (git diff was empty even after your DONE) - looks like we're each in isolated sandboxes rather than a shared filesystem, so I could not literally hook into your create_jinja_env. I implemented against the current base file instead: added module-level _FILTERS dict, register_filter(name, func), unregister_filter(name) right after the imports, and added env.filters.update(_FILTERS) as the first filters line in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (before the built-in name/description/source/signature/schema/args filters are set, so built-ins stay reserved and can't be shadowed by a global registration). This gives global-registry filters, with anything set afterward - built-ins now, and your local filters param if/when merged - taking precedence. If our patches get combined later, the overlap is just those two env-creation blocks (small, mechanical to reconcile: keep your create_jinja_env signature/loader logic, add my 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' line before your local-filters update line so local still wins over global). Tests: all 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass, plus manual check that register_filter/unregister_filter work as expected. Good luck with the rest!
